threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think calling it a 'corner shop' is normal when everyone else calls it 'the deli' or 'the local'.
Last night on the 133, a guy tried to order a kebab from the driver through the window. We all just cracked up; it’s the little things that keep you going!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a whole family of ducks waddling down Karl-Marx-Straße like they owned the place. Only in Neukölln do you get that kind of vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it's normal to spot a techno party outside a kebab shop at 3 AM. The smell of döner mixes with thumping bass—truly a Berlin moment.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they know to avoid the Civic Center bus stop at rush hour—especially after a loud bang or dog on the tracks.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw someone feeding the pigeons at Civic Center while Muni idled behind a dog on the tracks. Only in SF do delays come with a side of street performance.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but the real vibe’s in the narrow streets, where you can still find locals arguing over football in the evenings.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with the ticket machines at the metro, looking puzzled, while locals breeze past, tapping their cards effortlessly.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the tube' instead of just 'the Overground' like the rest of us. You're definitely new around here.
Last night on the 59, a woman sang Adele at the top of her lungs, while a trio played a game of cards in the back. Perfect Brixton vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw the lady at Sonnenallee knitting on the tram and chatting about her cat with a stranger. Pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a café that serves coffee in reclaimed lightbulbs. Quirky and eco-friendly, it’s like sipping art!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they know the difference between a burrito and a Mission-style burrito. It’s all in the beans, man.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the bus while someone else was having an existential crisis about the next stop. Classic Muni magic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Park Güell, but they miss the quiet charm of Plaça de la Vila, where locals unwind with a vermut on Saturdays.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their baffled look while staring at the tram schedule, wondering why buses aren't just as punctual.
